data.title: Meteoric Resources's Stock Price Surge May Help Funding Efforts, Says Euroz Hartleys
data.description:
Meteoric Resources' share surge on Tuesday is likely due to rising interest in rare earth companies after recent export restrictions from China and new US government plans to build a strategic supply of rare earth materials, Euroz Hartleys said in a Tuesday note.

The company on Tuesday reported a mineral resource estimate at the Barro do Pacu license within the Caldera project in Brazil of 389 million tonnes at 2,204 parts-per-million grade of total rare earth oxides, including 77 million tonnes of indicated resources at a grade of 2,917 ppm TREO.

Euroz Hartley believes the stock surge can help the company's funding efforts.

The research firm said that Meteoric Resources' price target is currently under review.

the mining firm's shares surged 29% on market close.

data.title: UK Employment Growth Hits 5-Month High
data.description:
The number of employed people in the United Kingdom jumped by 206 thousand in the three months to February 2025, following a 144 thousand advance in the previous period.

This marks the highest job creation since the three months to September last year, driven by a rise in full-time employment.

Compared to the same period a year earlier, employment surged by 683 thousand, prompted by increases in both employees and self-employed workers.

Meanwhile, the employment rate for those aged 16 to 64 rose to 75.1%, up by 0.2 percentage point from the previous period.

data.title: Applied Materials buys 9% stake in advanced packaging firm BESI
data.description:
Applied Materials has bought a 9% stake in BE Semiconductor industries (BESI) , the U.S.-based computer chip equipment supplier said on Monday.

The Dutch semiconductor advanced packaging firm produces the world's most accurate hybrid bonding tool, a critical chip technology allowing two chips to be bonded directly on top of each other.

"I think that this is an astute move by (Applied Materials) to foster closer collaboration between the two companies," Timm Schulze-Melander, analyst at Redburn Atlantic, said in an email.

The purchase makes it clear Applied is not developing an alternative to BESI's hybrid bonding tech, he added.

Unlike most semiconductor packaging steps, hybrid bonding occurs much closer to the chip's production line and closer to Applied Materials' tools.

"I think shareholders will be extremely excited and will assume that Applied will eventually want to buy the entire company," Michael Roeg from Degroof Petercam said in an emailed comment.

Applied Materials said it did not intend to seek board representation at BESI.

Hybrid bonding is used on the world's most advanced chips, like AMD's X3D line, where the memory chip is bonded onto the processor by TSMC .

data.title: UK recruiter Robert Walters reports 16% drop in first quarter net fee income
data.description:
British recruiter Robert Walters on Tuesday reported a 16% fall in first-quarter net fee income, as challenging macro-economic conditions disrupt the global hiring market.

Mounting economic uncertainties, exacerbated by U.S. tariffs-incited trade wars, and strained political conditions in Europe have dampened employer confidence, leading to a slowdown in permanent and temporary hiring.

For the quarter ended March 31, the company reported net fee income of 67.3 million pounds ($88.84 million) compared to 81.3 million pounds in the prior year period.

data.title: Frasers Group Agrees Long-Term Strategic Partnership With Accent Group
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Frasers Group said it has agreed a long-term strategic retail partnership with Australian footwear and fashion retailer Accent Group, aimed at launching the Sports Direct brand in Australia and New Zealand.

The U.K. sports-fashion retailer on Tuesday said the 25-year agreement will see it increase its shareholding in Accent Group by 5% to 19.57%. It added that the funds from the subscription--around 29 million pounds ($38.3 million)--will be used to fund the initial roll-out of the Sports Direct business in the region.

As part of the agreement, Accent Group acquired Frasers' discount online fashion marketplace MySale.

Frasers took a strategic stake in Accent, which operates its own chains and distributes brands including Timberland, Hoka and Vans, in August 2024. Former Sports Direct CEO Dave Forsey subsequently joined Accent's board.

Accent will initially roll-out at least 50 Sports Direct stores, plus online, over the first six years, with both companies seeing the potential to increase this to up to 100 stores in the region over time.

"This reaffirms our commitment to drive growth of the Sports Direct brand internationally and marks a significant step forward in our ambition to becoming the leading global sporting goods retailer," Frasers said.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: B&M European Value Retail Expects Key Earnings Figure Above Guided Midpoint
data.description:
By Ian Walker

B&M European Value Retail said it expects to report that a preferred metric for fiscal 2025 came in above the previously guided midpoint, and that it is making progress on the search for a new chief executive.

The FTSE 250 convenience retailer said Tuesday that group revenue for the year ended March 29 rose 3.7% to 5.6 billion pounds ($7.39 billion). Within this, B&M UK revenue was 4.48 billion pounds, while B&M France revenue at 543 million pounds and Heron Foods revenue at 546 million pounds.

On Feb. 24 the company cut its guidance for adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization--a preferred metric that strips out exceptional and other one-off items--for the year to a range of between 605 million pounds and 625 million pounds.

Prior to that, it had forecast adjusted Ebitda of between 620 million and 650 million pounds. Adjusted Ebitda for fiscal 2024 was 629 million pounds.

The company said it expects to make an announcement on its new CEO within weeks. It said on Feb. 25 that Alex Russo would retire on April 30 and that its search for a replacement was at an advanced stage.

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: What are the main events for today?
data.description:
In the European session, the highlight was the UK employment report. The data was mixed but it didn't change anything for the BoE anyway. All the data in April is old news and will be mostly ignored by the market. The only thing I can see having an impact is a surge in US Jobless Claims to new cycle highs. Looking ahead, we will also get the German ZEW data but again, it's unlikely to have any impact.

In the American session, the highlight is the Canadian CPI report. The underlying inflation rate in Canada has been creeping up recently which is making the BoC's job harder amid slowing growth due to tariffs.

12:30 GMT/08:30 ET - Canada March CPI

The Canadian CPI Y/Y is expected at 2.6% vs. 2.6% prior, while the M/M reading is seen at 0.6% vs. 1.1% prior. The Trimmed-Mean CPI Y/Y is expected at 3.0% vs. 2.9% prior, while the Median CPI Y/Y is seen at 3.0% vs. 2.9% prior.

Inflation has been moving higher recently after the aggressive BoC easing, and the tariffs are expected to keep inflation higher while weighing on growth. The market sees 44 bps of easing by year-end with a 61% probability that the central bank will hold rates unchanged this week.

Canada Inflation Measures

Central bank speakers:

  • 15:35 GMT/11:35 ET - Fed's Barkin (neutral - non voter)

data.title: Beiersdorf's Q1 Group Sales Rise
data.description:
Beiersdorf said Tuesday its group sales for the three months ended March 31 grew year over year.

The German personal care products manufacturers' group sales were 2.69 billion euros, compared with 2.60 billion euros a year ago and the FactSet-compiled consensus estimate of 2.66 billion euros.

For full-year 2025, the company affirmed expectations for organic sales growth in the 4% to 6% range.

data.title: Robert Walters Flags Uncertainties Due to Global Tariffs; Fee Income Declines
data.description:
By Michael Susin

Robert Walters reported a drop in income for the first quarter and warned that uncertainties regarding global tariffs will weigh near term.

The British recruitment company said Tuesday that net fee income fell 16% on a constant currency basis to 67.3 million pounds ($88.8 million), with declines across all regions.

The performance was mainly dragged by the Asia-Pacific region, its key income contributor, which fell 15% to 27.4 million pounds due to more cautious hiring behavior for permanent positions.

Europe's net fee income fell 22% to 21.9 million pounds, particularly affected by specialist recruitment.

"Increased uncertainty regarding the flow of global trade due to tariffs is likely to be a further headwind to client and candidate confidence in the near term--limiting visibility on the outlook for the balance of the year at the present time," Chief Executive Toby Fowlston said.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: Wise Posts Higher Underlying Income, Cross-border Volume for Fiscal Q4
data.description:
Wise on Tuesday reported a rise in cross-border volume and underlying income for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025.

The money transfer company's underlying income was 350.4 million pounds sterling for the quarter ended March 31, compared with 309.5 million pounds a year ago.

Cross-border volume totaled 39.1 billion pounds, compared with 30.6 billion pounds.

data.title: TotalEnergies 1Q Integrated Power Results Expected Between $450M and $500M
Ericsson Posts Rise in North American Network Spending Ahead of TariffsUpdate
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

STOCKHOLM--Ericsson's first-quarter sales were boosted by some North American customers rushing to buy network equipment as they moved forward investments ahead of planned U.S. import tariffs.

The Swedish telecommunications-equipment company said Tuesday that its key network business posted a 6% rise in network sales overall in the period, with strong growth in North America offsetting significant declines in India as well as weakness in the Middle East and Africa.

North American network sales benefited from previous contract wins and accelerated network investments by other customers, in part reflecting tariff uncertainty, it said.

However, import duties are likely to have a slight hit on margins in the second quarter, it added.

Tariffs on U.S. imports of foreign goods were due to begin in early April, but President Trump implemented a 90-day pause on most countries except China.

Ericsson said previously that its broad supply-chain footprint should help it navigate potential tariffs on goods imported to the U.S., if the new U.S. administration moves ahead with them.

The company has production in North America, Latin America, Europe, Asia and India, and management has said it has the opportunity to move production between different sites.

But speaking to business channel CNBC on Tuesday, Chief Executive Borje Ekholm said the effect from tariffs was more likely to be felt from its sourcing of components.

"What I think is important to highlight, if we look at tariffs, it's less on the manufacturing side as it is actually on components because we need to source components globally and also have long-term contracts, long-term relationships where we need to source."

In the evolving global trade landscape and volatility, Ericsson said it continues to focus on controlling what it can control and delivering to customers.

"We are not immune, but we are resilient, with well-diversified production close to the customer and the flexibility to adapt to changing conditions over time," Ekholm said in a statement.

The company reported an adjusted gross margin in the networks unit of 51.0%, above guidance of 47% to 49%, thanks to selling more profitable goods to higher-margin countries. Improved supply-chain efficiencies and cost-reduction measures also helped.

Based on its current assessment of announced tariffs, and including a negative impact from tariffs estimated at around one percentage point, adjusted gross margin in the second quarter is expected to be in the range of 48% to 50%.

Network sales growth in the second quarter is expected to be broadly similar to three-year average seasonality.

Net profit attributable to shareholders in the first quarter rose to 4.15 billion kronor ($424.9 million) from 2.56 billion kronor a year earlier, above the 2.41 billion kronor expected in a FactSet analyst poll.

Sales rose 3.2% to 55.03 billion kronor, shy of the 55.5 billion kronor FactSet estimate.

The company said restructuring charges rose to 300 million kronor in the quarter, mainly related to redundancies.

Restructuring charges for 2025 are still expected to remain at elevated levels, it said.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

Chunghwa Chemical Synthesis to Buy Back Up to 1 Million Shares
data.description:
Chunghwa Chemical Synthesis & Biotech plans to repurchase up to 1 million shares, or 1.29% of its total issued stock, between April 15 and June 14.

The shares will be bought from the market at a price range of NT$20 to NT$37 apiece, according to a Monday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: UK labour market shows slowdown signs in run-up to employer tax hike
data.description:
Britain's labour market showed signs of weakening in data published on Tuesday that suggested a hit to hiring in the run-up to this month's increase in a tax on employers, but wage growth remained strong.

The number of vacancies fell below their pre-COVID pandemic level for the first time since the three months to May 2021 in the three months to March.

Provisional data given by employers to the tax authorities showed the number of employees fell by 78,000 in March and February's figure was revised to show a drop of 8,000 compared with a previous estimate of a 21,000 gain.

While some of the figures published by the Office for National Statistics showed a weakening in hiring, pay growth was still fast, posing a conundrum for the Bank of England.

The BoE is trying to gauge whether inflation pressures in the labour market are easing sufficiently for it to continue cutting interest rates. It is also watching for the economic impact of U.S. President Donald Trump's trade tariffs.

Average weekly earnings, excluding bonuses, rose by 5.9% in the three months to February compared with the same period a year earlier, faster than a revised 5.8% increase in the three months to January.

Private-sector pay excluding bonuses - a gauge of domestic inflation pressure watched closely by the BoE - rose by 5.9%, compared with the same period a year earlier, unchanged from the pace in the three months to January.

A Reuters poll of economists had pointed to growth of 6.0% in both measures of basic pay.

data.title: UK Total Pay Growth Steady at 5.6%
data.description:
Total average weekly earnings in the UK which includes bonuses rose 5.6% year-on-year to GBP 716 per week in the three months to February 2025, the same as a downwardly revised 5.6% in the previous period and slightly below forecasts of 5.7%.

Wage growth eased in the private sector (5.6% vs 5.8%) but accelerated in the public sector (5.7%, the highest since the three months to May vs 5.2%).

Adjusted for inflation, total earnings grew by 1.9%, the same as in the previous period.

data.title: UK Unemployment Rate Stable at 4.4% in Quarter to February
data.description:
Britain's unemployment rate came in at 4.4% in the quarter to February, unchanged from the prior three-month period, the Office for National Statistics said Tuesday.

The latest reading matched the consensus estimate for the period.

Meanwhile, the UK employment rate came in at 75.1%.

data.title: Gufic Biosciences Appeals Tax Assessment Order
data.description:
Gufic Biosciences filed an appeal on Monday, before the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals), National Faceless Appeal Centre, Delhi, India, against a March 18 assessment order, according to a same-day filing.

The company said it contested the disallowance of certain deductions without sufficient opportunity for explanation.

The expected financial implication is 118.9 million Indian rupees, Gufic Biosciences said in the filing.

data.title: Britain's Frasers Group to launch Sports Direct in Australia, NZ
data.description:
British sportswear and fashion group Frasers Group on Tuesday said it has signed an agreement with Australian footwear retailer Accent Group to launch and operate Sports Direct across Australia and New Zealand.

Frasers Group, which is majority owned by billionaire Mike Ashley, retails sports and fitness footwear, clothing, and equipment under the Sports Direct brand and said it plans to open 100 stores in the region.

Apollo Micro Systems Bags New Orders Worth INR75.2 Million; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Electronic hardware and software solutions provider Apollo Micro Systems has secured orders worth 75.2 million Indian rupees from various clients, according to a Tuesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The clients include India's Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO), a public sector undertaking (PSU), and a private company.

The company has also been declared as the lowest bidder for orders worth 114.8 million rupees from DRDO and the PSU.

Shares of the company were up over 3% in recent trade.
